behavior_in_task_description.reason: Instruction names all affected files, the deprecated tokens (:filename, :slugorfilename, .Site.Data), replacements (:contentbasename, :slugorcontentbasename, hugo.Data), and the expected URL paths asserted in tests.

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction explicitly states the exact replacements: 'use :contentbasename instead of :filename', 'use :slugorcontentbasename instead of :slugorfilename', and 'use hugo.Data instead of .Site.Data' — this is the fix verbatim.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests check for the presence of the new tokens/strings and run Go tests; any correct fix using these replacements will pass.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Tests invoke `go test`, `go build`, `go vet`, `gofmt`, and `go mod verify` subprocesses in addition to text checks.

test_not_tautological.reason: The f2p tests run actual Go tests (TestPermalinks, TestHTMLFilesIsue11999, TestMultiHost, TestMount, TestPagePathDisablePathToLower) which must pass behaviorally.

behavior_in_tests.reason: Each behavior listed in instruction (token replacements, data access change, URL paths) is covered by corresponding tests.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are well-named with docstrings indicating f2p vs p2p categorization.

no_typos.reason: Filenames, commands, and tokens appear consistent between instruction and tests.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: Instruction names exact files and the exact tokens to replace ('use :contentbasename instead of :filename'), removing the localization/diagnosis step that would otherwise be part of the task.
